transcript.clean_text: the 9th of july 2021 a day for mark caverdish to rewrite the history books and put his name alongside that of eddie merks the greatest male cyclist of all time on 34 stage wins of the tour de france meds won his last stage in 1975 mark cavendish is first on this very date yes the 9th of july 2008 today sage will take the riders from neem to carcassonne 220 kilometers but with the threat of crosswinds a lot of heat about it was never nailed on to be a bunch sprint after an extremely active and nervous first 25 kilometers three riders managed to sneak away they were omar goldstein of israel's startup nation piolator of total energy and sean bennett running for quebec next hash the peliton calmed down behind and cavendish's the koenig quickstep team took control alongside the other sprint challengers from alpacine fenix the break was allowed to get a very good gap just one point for the king of the mountains on offer when it came to the intermediate sprint there were 13 points on offer sony colbrelli took most of them canvadis not too bothered it all looked to be heading towards a predictable finish until philippe gilbert attacked with 66 k's to go and then behind there was an almighty crash several riders down unfortunately several also had to abandon including both hamilton and yates of the bike exchange team sirenclaw was up from a ditch and it took a few riders time to get back the morning breakaway would finally be caught with just over 50 kilometers still to go had attacked the race but a lot of the teams came to the front inside the final 20 kilometers with the wind blowing from the right-hand side richard cara pass even trying to split the race with michal kviatkowski cavedish the rest followed it was chaotic it was difficult and inside the final four kilometers there were twists and turns more than you could count the koenig quickstep though did take over they took the race inside the final couple of turns depleted with the loss of the clef to the crash they would go out with ballerini then mako would follow but all eyes on cavendish as the final couple of hundred meters approached garcia cortina attacking trying to take it early in the sprint kavadish waiting on the wheel of mickey and marco merkel so good in the sprints the best lead-out man in the world in perhaps the best lead out train ever assembled cavendish would come around him it would be a decent quick step one two and an amazing moment at a tour de france for the ages history was made the record books were altered and right alongside eddie merks now stands mark cavendish 34 stage wins at the tour de france kavadish equaling the all-time record that's four at this tour five for the team including ala philippe's opening day win they've had the yellow jersey too they still have the green jersey the current quickstep and mark cavendish are on fire at this tour de france cavanese and marco with a one-two phillips and having to settle for third garcia cortina and danny from popple making up the top five a few worries in the winds towards the end for bogatya but he negotiated them well he still leads by five minutes and 18 seconds ningago still in third behind uran the day however belonging to mark caverdish back at the tour de france nine months after it looked as though he might be having to retire without a team he also has a 101 point lead in the green jersey competition with possibly two sprints left he could well break the all-time record whisper it quietly maybe on the champs-elysees before we get to that though it's back to the mountains and back to the gc battle stage 14 183.7 kilometers bonus seconds towards the end it's up and down all day another unpredictable day awaits and perhaps a challenge to today pogaccha the breakaway specialists will be looking at this as an opportunity too the tour de france rolls on day 14 promises to be another big day out you
